task_completion.description: A curve markup exists in the scene.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A curve markup is visible in the 2D axial viewer of the medical imaging software.

task_completion.description: The curve markup is a closed loop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The ends of the curve are connected, forming a continuous closed shape without any open gaps.

task_completion.description: The curve has at least 10 control points.
task_completion.visual_evidence: There are 10 or more distinct control points (nodes/dots) visible along the perimeter of the curve.
The Markups or Data module UI shows the curve's name exactly as 'GTV_Tumor_Margin'.

task_completion.description: The curve is located on the visible tumor in an axial slice.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The curve is drawn on an axial (horizontal/top-down) slice view, surrounding the hyperintense (bright) tumor region characteristic of a FLAIR MRI.

task_completion.description: The curve accurately outlines the visible tumor margins.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The curve closely follows the irregular boundaries of the bright tumor tissue, rather than being a generic, loose, or poorly fitted shape.

integrity.description: The agent used the application GUI to create the markup.
integrity.visual_evidence: The medical imaging software (e.g., 3D Slicer) interface is visible with active toolbars or modules, rather than just a terminal window executing a script.

integrity.description: The curve is a genuine software markup object.
integrity.visual_evidence: The curve appears as a native markup object in the software's UI (e.g., listed in the Markups module hierarchy) and not as a drawn overlay from an external paint tool.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual BraTS MRI data.
integrity.visual_evidence: The viewer shows a genuine brain MRI scan with a visible tumor, not a blank screen, dummy image, or text file.
